page.content: In the period of 2Q FY2025, the Tax Revenue Composition indicated a total revenue of CAD 45.2M from Income Tax, CAD 30.8M from Sales Tax, CAD 12.4M from Property Tax, and CAD 7.6M from Other Taxes. For Q1 FY2025, the values were CAD 42.1M for Income Tax, CAD 29.3M for Sales Tax, CAD 11.9M for Property Tax, and CAD 6.7M for Other Taxes.

lines.text: In the period of 2Q FY2025, the Tax Revenue Composition indicated a total revenue of CAD 45.2M from Income Tax, CAD
30.8M from Sales Tax, CAD 12.4M from Property Tax, and CAD 7.6M from Other Taxes. For Q1 FY2025, the values were
CAD 42.1M for Income Tax, CAD 29.3M for Sales Tax, CAD 11.9M for Property Tax, and CAD 6.7M for Other Taxes.

page.content: The prior year’s Q2 FY2024 saw a total of CAD 40.5M in Income Tax, CAD 28.6M in Sales Tax, CAD 10.5M in Property Tax, and CAD 6.7M in Other Taxes, while Q1 FY2024 recorded CAD 39.8M in Income Tax, CAD 27.7M in Sales Tax, CAD 10.2M in Property Tax, and CAD 5.9M in Other Taxes.

lines.text: The prior year’s Q2 FY2024 saw a total of CAD 40.5M in Income Tax, CAD 28.6M in Sales Tax, CAD 10.5M in Property Tax,
and CAD 6.7M in Other Taxes, while Q1 FY2024 recorded CAD 39.8M in Income Tax, CAD 27.7M in Sales Tax, CAD 10.2M
in Property Tax, and CAD 5.9M in Other Taxes.

page.content: The growth rates for these tax categories were 5.1% for Income Tax, 5.1% for Sales Tax, 11.4% for Property Tax, and 3.7% for Other Taxes. In terms of Social Welfare Metrics, the Total Expenditure in 2025 Q2 was CAD 150B, with Per Capita Spending at $4,000, Percentage of GDP at 6.5%, Welfare Index at 75.2, Funding Sources at 40.0%, and Debt to GDP Ratio at 40.0%. In 2024 Q2, the figures were CAD 140B, $3,800, 6.2%, 73.5, and 38.5%, while in 2023 Q2, they were CAD 130B, $3,600, 5.8%, 71.0, and 37.0%, with 2022 Q2 reporting CAD 120B, $3,400, 5.5%, 68.5, and 35.5%. The 2021 Q2 metrics were CAD 110B, $3,200, 5.0%, 65.0, and 34.0%, while 2020 Q2 recorded CAD 100B, $3,000, 4.5%, 62.0, and 32.5%.
